title: Transport

vocabulary.meaning: the way from one place to another
something used for carrying people or goods
the movement of vehicles on roads
to travel regularly between home and work or school
used by everyone, not by one person only

questions: Which is your favourite means of private transport?
What skills do you need to practise to pass the driving test in your country? Did you pass your test first time? What do you find most difficult about driving?
Which is your favourite kind of public transport - buses or trains? ships or planes?
Are you in favour of more road building to create more space for the private motorist in your country?
Do you think that more freight should be sent by rail? Why are companies reluctant to send their goods this way? What needs to be done to change their minds?
What annoys you most about other drivers?
Have you ever broken the speed limit, driven the wrong way along a one-way street, overtaken on the inside, driven while drunk, etc?
How would your life be different if cars were banned? Can you think of three ways in which you would benefit from the banning of cars?
What do you think should be done to increase road safety?
At what age, or in what circumstances, should people stop driving for good?
What can be done to encourage people to use their cars less and public transport more?
Winston Churchill said "Cars are the curse of the 20th century". To what extent do you think they will be the curse of the 21st century too?
